name.en: Azorean Sweet Bread (Pão Doce / Massa Sovada)
name.es: Pan dulce azoriano (Pão Doce / Massa Sovada)
name.fr: Pain sucré açorien (Pão Doce / Massa Sovada)

description.en: A pillowy, egg-gold Azorean round loaf, sweetened with sugar and lemon zest, baked for the Holy Ghost festivals that light up Azorean summers. Emigrants carried the dough to Hawaii, where it became the ancestor of Hawaiian sweet bread.
description.de: Ein locker-goldenes Azoren-Rundbrot mit Zucker und Zitronenschale, gebacken zu den Feiern des Heiligen Geistes, die den Sommer auf den Azoren prägen. Auswanderer brachten den Teig nach Hawaii, wo daraus das Hawaiian Sweet Bread wurde.
description.es: Un pan redondo azoriano, dorado con huevo y perfumado con ralladura de limón, horneado para las fiestas del Espíritu Santo que animan los veranos de las Azores. Los emigrantes llevaron esta masa a Hawái, origen del pan dulce hawaiano.
description.fr: Un pain rond açorien doré à l'œuf, parfumé au zeste de citron, cuit pour les fêtes du Saint-Esprit qui rythment l'été aux Açores. Les émigrants ont emporté cette pâte à Hawaï, où elle est devenue l'ancêtre du pain sucré hawaïen.

origin.note: Called massa sovada ('kneaded dough') in the Azores; Azorean immigrants carried it to Hawaii in the late 1800s, where it evolved into Hawaiian sweet bread and, later, King's Hawaiian rolls.

ingredients.note: mashed sweet potato, a traditional Azorean addition

yield.shape: large round loaf, egg-washed and often cross-scored

keeping.note: Best the day it's baked; toasts well or makes fine French toast from day two on.

method.title: Wake the yeast
method.summary: Dissolve the fresh yeast in the warm water with a pinch of the sugar and let it foam for about 10 minutes.

method.title: Build the dough
method.summary: Beat the eggs with the sugar and lemon zest until pale, then work in the flour, warmed milk and mashed sweet potato along with the yeast mixture.

method.title: Knead in butter and salt
method.summary: Knead well past the point the dough first comes together, then work in the softened butter and salt until the dough turns glossy, smooth and elastic - this bread wants a longer knead than a plain loaf.

method.title: Bulk rise
method.summary: Cover and let rise at room temperature until roughly doubled, about 3 hours.

method.title: Shape
method.summary: Deflate gently and shape into one large round loaf on a lined baking sheet.

method.title: Final proof
method.summary: Cover loosely and proof until visibly puffy, about 90 minutes.

method.title: Bake
method.summary: Brush with beaten egg, optionally score a shallow cross on top, and bake at 180°C for about 45 minutes until deeply golden.

method.title: Cool
method.summary: Cool completely on a rack before slicing; the crumb is tender and slightly stringy when pulled apart.

source.adaptation: Ratios composited from this Azorean recipe with a moderate hydration increase; the overnight starter was simplified to one stage. Hawaii lineage per Wikipedia's Portuguese sweet bread entry.
